profile_method: prewarm TileLang kernels in the same container, then rocprofv3 --pmc SQ_WAVES SQ_INSTS_LDS SQ_INSTS_VMEM_RD SQ_INSTS_VMEM_WR SQ_LDS_BANK_CONFLICT with kernel include regex for mfma_panel_atomic_final_limited_bshd_source_hsk_bf16_scaled_prim_kernel
read: The source-fed TileLang kernel is not dominated by a large count of VMEM write instructions in SQ counters. Per dispatch it issues ~217M LDS instructions and ~331M LDS bank-conflict events, versus ~39M VMEM read instructions and ~1.05M VMEM write instructions. That points the next kernel rewrite at the in-CTA LDS active-group accumulation/reduction layout as well as final atomics, not only global write count.

tcc_counter_pass.note: SSH timed out during the TCC pass and no counter CSV or benchmark JSON was left behind; do not use it as evidence.

next: Try a TileLang/HIP variant that changes the local active-group accumulator layout to reduce LDS bank conflicts: e.g., split active rows across smaller panels, swizzle LDS rows/banks, or do warp-local reductions before shared-memory accumulation. Keep HSK BF16 source feeding and cached/static routing; do not spend effort on the materialized pack bridge.
